gi_source.title: Crab meat — glycemic index reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index for crab meat is effectively zero as it contains no glycemic carbohydrates; set to 1 per Veritas v2 convention for zero-GI foods. gi_category set to low.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume crab in moderation due to its natural sodium content (317 mg per 100 g), particularly when prepared with added salt, soy sauce, or other high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Cooked crab is a suitable protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains virtually no carbohydrates and thus has a negligible impact on postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: As a lean protein source with low saturated fat (0.13 g per 100 g), cooked crab can be incorporated into a heart-healthy diet, though its cholesterol content (85 mg per 100 g) should be considered within overall daily dietary intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cooked crab, served as a main dish in Singaporean Chinese cuisine, refers to the prepared meat of various crab species including blue crab, mud crab, or dungeness crab. It is typically steamed, grilled, boiled, or stir-fried, sometimes with aromatic sauces such as chili, black pepper, or salted egg. The flesh is tender, sweet, and succulent, offering a distinct oceanic flavour. Nutritionally, cooked crab is an excellent source of lean protein (approximately 18 g per 100 g) with very low fat and virtually no carbohydrates, making it highly suitable for blood sugar management. It provides notable micronutrients including vitamin B12, selenium, zinc, and phosphorus. The moderate sodium content (317 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. It is a popular celebratory seafood dish in Singapore, often enjoyed in communal settings at seafood restaurants.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from a generic cooked blue crab entry in USDA FoodData Central, as specific data for 'Combo Crab (L) 2PCS' from The Seafood Market is not available in any approved source. GI is for generic crab meat. The dish name implies a specific restaurant offering, but the core food is cooked crab.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero for cooked crab meat as it is a pure animal protein with no starchy components. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same rationale. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as crab meat contains no plant-derived fibre. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central for this entry. Glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas v2 convention. All micronutrient values are reported from USDA FoodData Central for 'Crab, blue, cooked, moist heat' (FDC ID 172088). The serving size of 200 g represents approximately one portion of a large crab (500–600 g whole weight yields roughly 30–40% edible meat).
